survivors_flagged.one_line: China NMPA accepted F351 (hydronidone) NDA under priority review for conditional approval in CHB liver fibrosis — a dated forward regulatory binary; US IND filing also targeted end-2026.

Copper World final air-quality permit cleared; definitive feasibility study + project-sanctioning decision dated to 2026, plus Arizona Sonoran acquisition — a real dated development milestone but more capex-greenlight than mispriced special-sit.
Definitive all-stock reverse merger with Hornbeck; HSR early termination granted 6/11/26, S-4 filed, expected close H2 2026 (becomes HOS, Hornbeck holders 55%) — live dated structural situation.
Aerospace spin-off on track to complete H2 2026 (Solstice already spun 10/25); large-cap so asymmetry modest, but a real dated forward separation.
Live MA star-ratings binary: 5th-Circuit appeal pending w/ fresh favorable Clover precedent (May-27-2026) already forcing CMS to recalc QBP ratings; mispriced legal asymmetry on 2026 bonus payments.
Definitive all-stock merger-of-equals with Olin announced Jun-15-2026 (0.5476 OLN/HUN, new 'OlinHuntsman'); both boards approved, pending shareholder/antitrust close — clean dated merger-arb.
Live sale process in 2nd round (Blackstone/Centerbridge DD) + Donerail $35 all-cash bid and proxy fight to oust CEO at 2026 AGM — multi-gate, dated, forward.
Dated sBLA PDUFA Jan 6 2027 for ANKTIVA papillary NMIBC, but far out and franchise already approved/re-rated; asymmetry not clearly mispriced.
Darovasertib uveal-melanoma Phase 2/3 hit (that re-rate fired); forward dated catalyst is the H2-2026 NDA submission under FDA Real-Time Oncology Review, with Servier partnership de-risking.

Active portfolio-reshape/deleveraging: Pharma Solutions sale closed, Soy Crush/Lecithin agreed, Food Ingredients under strategic review, targeting sub-3.0x leverage; forward but partly priced and milestone-paced rather than one hard binary.

LIVE deal: MTN to acquire IHS at $8.50/sh cash (~$6.2B EV) under a signed Feb-17-2026 merger agreement, closing expected 2026 pending Nigerian regulatory + shareholder approvals — clean merger-arb (stock ~$8.20 vs $8.50 leaves spread/regulatory risk to underwrite).
Commercial-stage (KIMMTRAK) with a dated forward binary: brenetafusp TEBE-AM Phase 3 cutaneous-melanoma topline expected as early as 2H-2026, plus tebentafusp 5-yr OS data 1H-2026.

Varegacestat (desmoid) NDA submitted April 2026 off positive Phase 3 RINGSIDE; forward FDA decision is a dated binary (orphan indication, limited competition), though exact PDUFA date not yet disclosed.
PRAME cell-therapy: SUPRAME Phase 3 interim PFS analysis is event-triggered (~200 pts) with BLA targeted 1Q-2027, so a real dated binary exists but not imminent in 2026; well-funded into 2028.

verified.catalyst: China's NMPA accepted the NDA for F351 (hydronidone) under priority review for CHB-induced liver fibrosis — a potential first-in-world fibrosis-reversal approval — with a US IND filing also targeted by end-2026.
verified.dated_milestone: NMPA NDA accepted under priority review 2026-05-12 (priority review granted 2026-03-17); approval decision pending — China priority review (~130 working days from acceptance) implies a decision window ~Q4 2026/early 2027; US IND filing target end-2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: China NMPA/CDE) + G2 (concrete commitment: priority-review NDA accepted; US IND committed by end-2026)
verified.edge: H — stock at $6.26 sits in its 52-week-low zone (low $5.44) and is ~16% below the 200d avg ($7.45), having drifted lower since the May 12 acceptance; a discrete, regulator-driven first-in-world fibrosis-reversal approval is not reflected in a declining tape at a ~$573M cap.
verified.primary_source: Gyre Therapeutics press release, "NMPA Acceptance of NDA for F351 (hydronidone)," GlobeNewswire, 2026-05-12 (priority-review grant: GlobeNewswire 2026-03-17)
verified.kill_fact: Primary GlobeNewswire/Gyre IR releases confirm every load-bearing fact: NMPA/CDE ACCEPTED the F351 (hydronidone) NDA for CHB-induced liver fibrosis on 2026-05-12, after granting PRIORITY REVIEW on 2026-03-17; the May 12 release states verbatim 'Gyre plans to file an Investigational New Drug (IND) application in the U.S. by the end of 2026.' Catalyst is FORWARD (approval decision pending, not fired), dated, and non-macro — passes G1+G2. The tape sits in a 52-week-low zone (low ~$5.44; investing.com flagged a $8.26 52-wk low in early June 2026), so the binary is unpriced/tradeable on NASDAQ. Honest gaps the line already discloses: company gave NO explicit NMPA decision date (approval timing is inferred from standard China priority-review duration, ~6-9mo) and NO peak-sales figure, so G3 (terms/magnitude) is only partially met. The 'first-in-world fibrosis-reversal' framing is supported by the priority-review release. No hidden disqualifier found.
verified.analysis: Verified real, named, and dated: NMPA/CDE accepted the F351 NDA on 2026-05-12 after granting priority review on 2026-03-17, with management committing to a US IND filing by end-2026 (GlobeNewswire releases). The approval decision is still pending (not fired) and the tape has drifted to a 52-week-low zone post-acceptance, so the binary is unpriced — passing G1+G2 as a forward, dated, non-macro regulatory catalyst. Gap: the company stated no explicit NMPA decision date and gave no peak-sales figure (G3 not cleanly met), so the exact approval timing is inferred from standard China priority-review duration rather than a company-confirmed date.

verified.bloom_gates: G2 (partial) — a concrete, self-directed capex/build commitment is dated, but it is not a named third-party counterparty (G1) nor a specific currently-unpriced figure (G3); the $1.7B economics and Mitsubishi $600M/30% JV are already disclosed and modeled.
verified.edge: L — the two splashy events (air-quality permit Jan-2025, Arizona Sonoran close Jun-24-2026) have already fired/re-rated; the remaining DFS+sanction is a known, self-funded capex-greenlight on a documented project, and outcome is heavily a copper-price macro bet. Price $22.85 sits mid 52wk range ($8.93–$32.15), above 200d avg — no dislocation.
verified.primary_source: Hudbay press releases: Air Quality Permit (globenewswire, Jan 2, 2025); Arizona Sonoran acquisition close (hudbayminerals.com / globenewswire, Jun 24, 2026); Q4/FY2025 results + Mitsubishi $600M JV close confirming DFS mid-2026 & sanction 2026 (hudbayminerals.com, FY2025 release / INN, Jan 2026)
verified.analysis: The headline items are fired, not forward: the final air-quality permit cleared Jan 2, 2025 and the all-share Arizona Sonoran acquisition closed June 24, 2026 (~$1.48B), both already re-rated into the stock. The only genuinely forward, dated milestone is the Copper World DFS (on track mid-2026) and the project-sanctioning/FID decision expected in 2H 2026 — but this is a self-funded capex-greenlight on a fully-permitted, Mitsubishi-JV-funded project whose $1.7B economics are already in models, so it lacks an unpriced figure or external counterparty trigger and its payoff leans on copper prices (a macro bet). Sound development name and a legitimate dated catalyst to track, but it is WATCH-grade, not an ACTIVE mispriced special-situation.

verified.catalyst: All-stock reverse merger with Hornbeck (HLX is the surviving listed entity, renames to HOS; Hornbeck holders end ~55%, Helix ~45%) advancing toward an H2-2026 close after FTC HSR early termination and S-4 filing.
verified.dated_milestone: S-4 filed 2026-06-04; FTC HSR early termination granted 2026-06-11; next dated gate = Helix special shareholder vote (date still blank "___, 2026" in the S-4), then expected close H2 2026.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty: Hornbeck Offshore) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed Merger Agreement, fixed 10.27167 exchange ratio, S-4 filed, HSR cleared). G3 FAILS — no specific currently-unpriced figure disclosed (no synergy $ or defined arb spread; all-stock fixed-ratio deal has no per-share takeout premium to capture).
verified.edge: L — the dated structure is real and unfired, but as an all-stock fixed-exchange-ratio reverse merger there is no defined cash takeout spread to arb; the only "edge" is a directional bet on undisclosed post-merger synergies, and the stock ($8.80, vs $8.10 200d / $10.75 yr-high) already trades on deal mechanics.
verified.primary_source: SEC Form S-4 (filed 2026-06-04, EDGAR CIK 0000866829) + Helix 8-K on FTC HSR early termination granted 2026-06-11 (via StockTitan, accessed 2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The merger is real, named, signed, and dated: S-4 filed 6/4/26, FTC HSR early termination 6/11/26, close expected H2 2026 contingent on the still-unscheduled Helix shareholder vote and remaining approvals — so it clears the dated/forward/not-fired bar for WATCH. But it is NOT a true merger-arb opportunity: it is an all-stock reverse merger with a fixed exchange ratio, so HLX holders retain ~45% of the combined entity rather than receiving a defined cash takeout, leaving no priced spread to capture and no disclosed synergy figure (G3 unmet). The actual thesis collapses to a directional view on the combined offshore-vessel company's value, which is a sector/operational bet the priced tape largely reflects — hence below ACTIVE conviction.

verified.catalyst: Honeywell's spin-off of Honeywell Aerospace (HONA) — the final step of its three-way split — distributing 1 HONA share per 2 HON shares, completing at 12:01 a.m. NYC time on June 29, 2026 (today).

verified.primary_source: Honeywell 8-K / Form 10-12B Information Statement and press release "Honeywell Board of Directors Approves Spin-Off of Honeywell Aerospace," sec.gov/honeywell.com, dated 2026-06-15 (distribution effective 2026-06-29)
verified.analysis: The board has approved the spin, the June 15 record date has passed, when-issued HONAV trading began ~June 15, and the distribution itself completes at 12:01 a.m. today (June 29, 2026) with regular-way HONA trading starting today — so the catalyst is FIRING/FIRED, not a pending dated-forward event. The triage note ("on track to complete H2 2026") was correct directionally but is now stale: the event has arrived and is fully priced via the live when-issued market. Fails the FORWARD gate (re-rated, not unpriced) and the asymmetry is already gone, so this is NONE rather than ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: Humana's 5th Circuit appeal of its 2026 MA star-ratings downgrade, with the May-27-2026 Clover precedent forcing a CMS recalc — but the recalc reportedly does not benefit Humana's largest contract.
verified.dated_milestone: CMS June-17-2026 voluntary 2027 QBP recalc cycle already ran (plan elections June 22, resubmitted bids due June 29, 2026); 5th Circuit appeal has NO scheduled oral-argument or ruling date.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 met (named counterparty: CMS/HHS, S.D. Ga. Clover ruling, 5th Cir.); G2/G3 FAIL — no concrete Humana-specific unpriced figure (Capstone: recalc "would likely not move the needle" on Humana's largest MA contract) and no dated forward milestone on the appeal.
verified.edge: L — the supposed asymmetry leans on the Clover-driven recalc, but that recalc largely fired June 17-29 2026 with effectively zero benefit to Humana's main contract; the residual 5th Cir. appeal is undated and 0/2 in prior rulings.
verified.primary_source: Fierce Healthcare / Healthcare Finance News, "CMS voluntarily recalculating 2027 MA QBP ratings" (June 17-18, 2026, citing Capstone on Humana's largest contract); Healthcare Dive, "Humana appeals MA star ratings loss to 5th Circuit" (Nov 25, 2025).
verified.analysis: All factual claims verify: appeal filed Nov-25-2025 / opening brief Feb-2026, Clover won May-27-2026, CMS announced a voluntary 2027 QBP recalc June-17-2026. But the thesis's value mechanism is refuted by its own catalyst — Capstone says the recalc "would likely not move the needle at all for Humana's largest MA contract" (UnitedHealthcare actually gets downgraded), so there is no specific currently-unpriced Humana figure (G3 fails). The recalc window has effectively fired (elections June-22, bid resubmission June-29-2026) and the remaining 5th Circuit appeal carries no dated milestone and an adverse 0/2 lower-court record, leaving no real dated forward catalyst.

verified.catalyst: Definitive all-stock merger-of-equals with Olin (0.5476 OLN per HUN, new "OlinHuntsman"), unanimously approved by both boards, pending dual shareholder votes and antitrust clearance toward a 1H-2027 close.
verified.dated_milestone: Deal announced Jun-16-2026; next gates = shareholder votes + HSR/antitrust clearance; targeted close 1H 2027.
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty Olin), G2 (definitive agreement, fixed 0.5476 ratio, unanimous board approval), G3 (currently-unpriced ~3.7% arb spread + >$400M identified synergies)
verified.edge: M — spread is real/dated but thin: 0.5476 x $20.49 OLN = $11.22 deal value vs HUN $10.82 = ~3.7% gross to a ~9-12mo close (~4-5% annualized). Fixed-ratio all-stock MoE means HUN carries full OLN price exposure (no cash floor), and two North American chlor-alkali/epoxy producers combining raises non-trivial antitrust overlap risk.
verified.primary_source: Huntsman/Olin joint press release "OLIN and HUNTSMAN Announce Transformative Merger of Equals" via huntsman.com + PRNewswire, Jun-16-2026; SEC Form 425/8-K filings same date.
verified.kill_fact: PRIMARY-CONFIRMED: PRNewswire release + SEC Form 425/8-K (Jun-16-2026) state all-stock merger of equals, fixed 0.5476 OLN per HUN, unanimous approval by both boards, combined "OlinHuntsman" (~54.5% OLN / ~45.5% HUN), close 1H-2027 pending both shareholder votes + regulatory/antitrust approvals. Live spread holds: HUN $10.815, OLN $20.505 -> implied 0.5476*$20.505=$11.23, gross spread ~3.8% (matches ~3.7% claim) = partially-fired, dated forward arb intact. Score 7 correct: fixed-ratio all-stock = full OLN exposure / no cash floor (both fell ~5% today), modest spread, live antitrust risk on overlapping NA chemicals players.
verified.analysis: Triage claim is verified and accurate: Olin (NYSE: OLN) and Huntsman announced a definitive all-stock merger of equals on Jun-16-2026 at a fixed 0.5476 OLN-per-HUN ratio, unanimously approved by both boards, creating "OlinHuntsman" with OLN holders ~54.5% / HUN ~45.5%; close targeted 1H 2027 pending both shareholder votes and antitrust clearance. At HUN $10.82 vs $11.22 implied deal value there is a ~3.7% gross spread, so the re-rate is only partially fired and a dated forward merger-arb remains. Score capped at 7 (not higher) because it is a fixed-ratio all-stock deal (HUN fully exposed to OLN, no downside cash floor), the spread is modest, and antitrust on two overlapping North American chemicals players is a live risk to the 1H-2027 timeline.

verified.kill_fact: All load-bearing facts independently verified: (1) Live multi-party auction confirmed — board entered second round April 2026 with Blackstone, Centerbridge, TPG in due diligence, Wells Fargo advising (Reuters, May 2026). (2) Donerail's $35 all-cash offer (Jan 13, 2026) confirmed and 'since raised' confirmed (revised figure undisclosed). (3) Proxy-fight leg correctly flagged as FIRED-and-FAILED: March 3, 2026 AGM re-elected CEO McGill 13,942,834 for vs 4,266,909 against (~76.5%) — matches the line's 13.9M/4.3M exactly. (4) Partial-pricing caveat is real and honestly stated: live price $36.93 (FMP) sits ABOVE the $35 floor and near the 52-wk high $38.14, so only the competitive clearing premium above floor is unpriced. (5) No binding-bid deadline disclosed; sources caution a deal is not guaranteed. G1 (named committed counterparties) satisfied, forward-not-fired, terms/dates/magnitudes accurate. ACTIVE/7 defensible: real dated forward special-situation, tempered by floor-in-tape and no timing anchor.
verified.analysis: HZO (MarineMax) is a genuine live, multi-party M&A auction: the board moved to a second round in April 2026 with Blackstone, Centerbridge and TPG conducting due diligence (Wells Fargo advising), alongside Donerail's $35 all-cash proposal that has since been raised, so G1 (named, committed counterparties) is clearly satisfied and the situation is forward, not a macro bet. The catch is partial pricing: at $36.93 the stock already trades above the $35 floor and near its 52-week high, so the only unpriced upside is the competitive auction-clearing premium above the floor; the proxy-fight-to-oust-CEO leg the triage flagged already fired and failed (McGill re-elected at the March 3, 2026 AGM, 13.9M vs 4.3M). Net: a real, dated, forward special-situation worth ACTIVE at 7, tempered by the floor being in the tape and no publicly disclosed binding-bid deadline to anchor timing.

verified.catalyst: FDA PDUFA decision on the ANKTIVA + BCG sBLA for BCG-unresponsive papillary-only NMIBC, a label expansion of an already-approved drug.
verified.dated_milestone: PDUFA action date Jan 6, 2027 (sBLA accepted May 2026)
verified.instrument: IBRX common equity (~$8.69, mkt cap ~$8-9B as of 2026-06-29)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 only (named counterparty = FDA, dated review); fails G2 (no concrete commitment beyond routine review) and G3 (no specific currently-unpriced figure)
verified.edge: L — dated and forward but asymmetry not mispriced: drug already FDA-approved (CIS, 2024), NCCN already added ANKTIVA+BCG for papillary-only as Cat 2A in Mar 2026 (so it's prescribable per-guideline pre-approval), approval rests on low-risk CIS extrapolation, and the stock has already re-rated (rev +700% FY25/+168% Q1'26) with recent moves driven by Russell 1000 inclusion, not the PDUFA
verified.primary_source: ImmunityBio PR / OncLive, FDA sBLA acceptance + PDUFA Jan 6, 2027 (May 2026); NCCN guideline update for papillary-only Cat 2A (Mar 2026)
verified.analysis: The Jan 6, 2027 PDUFA is real, dated, and forward (not yet fired), but the catalyst is a high-probability, low-surprise label expansion of an already-approved drug rather than a mispriced binary. NCCN already endorsed ANKTIVA+BCG for papillary-only NMIBC (Category 2A) in March 2026, so guideline-driven uptake can occur ahead of the formal label, and the franchise has already re-rated sharply on commercial traction — confirming the triage view that the asymmetry is not clearly unpriced. It clears only G1 (FDA review, dated); no concrete commitment (G2) or specific currently-unpriced figure (G3) was found, so it stays WATCH at a low score, far out and de-risked rather than an actionable edge.

verified.catalyst: Completion of the darovasertib+crizotinib NDA filing for first-line HLA-A*02:01-negative metastatic uveal melanoma under FDA's RTOR program, leading toward US approval/launch.
verified.dated_milestone: Full NDA filing completion targeted H2 2026 (first pre-submission already completed May 2026); PDUFA date not yet set.
verified.edge: M — strong de-risking Phase 2/3 data (PFS 6.9 vs 3.1mo, HR 0.42; ORR 37.1% vs 5.8%) + RTOR + Servier ex-US deal raise approval odds, but the data re-rate has largely fired (stock ~$36.5 near 52wk-high $39.3 vs low $20.5, +21% over 50DMA), the forward catalyst is a submission process-milestone not the approval, and no PDUFA date is set.
verified.primary_source: IDEAYA press release "to Initiate NDA Submission... under RTOR" (Apr 30, 2026) + Servier-IDEAYA license PR (Sep 2, 2025) + ASCO complete-data PR (Jun 1, 2026); FMP quote $36.46 (2026-06-29).
verified.analysis: The Phase 2/3 OptimUM-02 hit (topline Apr 13, 2026; complete ASCO data Jun 1, 2026) has already fired and is reflected in the price near 52-week highs. The genuinely forward, dated catalyst is completing the NDA filing in H2 2026 under FDA's accepted RTOR pathway, with a $100M Servier regulatory-approval milestone (of a $210M-upfront/$530M-total ex-US license) and retained US commercial rights in an indication with no approved first-line therapy. This is a real binary regulatory/commercial event with named counterparty (G1), concrete FDA commitment (G2) and a specific unpriced milestone figure (G3) — but it is a process milestone short of the actual approval and lacks a PDUFA date, so WATCH not ACTIVE.

verified.catalyst: IFF's portfolio reshape catalyst has fired: the Food Ingredients strategic review concluded in a signed agreement to sell the unit to CVC Capital Partners (~$4.3B EV / ~$3.8B net proceeds), announced June 1, 2026, with Pharma Solutions and Soy Crush/Lecithin already closed.
verified.dated_milestone: Food Ingredients/CVC sale signed 2026-06-01; regulatory close targeted end of Q2 2027; next update at Q2 2026 earnings call (~early Aug 2026).
verified.bloom_gates: None survive forward+unpriced. G1 (named counterparty=CVC) is satisfied but the deal is already SIGNED and re-rated; G2/G3 fail — no concrete unfired commitment or specific currently-unpriced figure remains. Leverage already 2.5x, below the sub-3.0x target.
verified.edge: L — the binary ("will they sell Food Ingredients / at what multiple") resolved on 2026-06-01 at a confirmed ~10x/$4.3B; sell-side reset targets (BofA $92, Citi $88, Barclays $90, Benchmark $100; consensus ~$93) and leverage is already inside the target. Residual is a 12-month regulatory close + buyback execution — milestone-paced and largely priced.
verified.primary_source: IFF/CVC press release "IFF Enters Into Agreement to Sell Its Food Ingredients Business to CVC" (ir.iff.com / cvc.com), 2026-06-01; corroborated by IFF 8-K (sec.gov d118591dex991) and Soy Crush/Lecithin completion release (businesswire/ir.iff.com), 2026-03-02.
verified.analysis: As of 2026-06-29 the triage's "strategic review / not one hard binary" premise is stale: the review concluded in a signed agreement to sell Food Ingredients to CVC Capital Partners for ~$4.3B EV (~10x EBITDA, ~$3.8B net cash), announced 2026-06-01, while Pharma Solutions (2025) and Soy Crush/Concentrates/Lecithin (closed to Bunge 2026-03-02) are already done. The market re-rated immediately — multiple Buy/OW analyst target resets to a ~$93 consensus — and net leverage is already 2.5x, below the sub-3.0x goal, so the deleveraging thesis is substantially priced. What remains (regulatory approvals to an end-of-Q2-2027 close, plus buyback/reinvestment of proceeds) is execution/milestone-paced, not a fresh unpriced forward binary, so this fails the strict forward-and-unpriced gate.

verified.catalyst: IHS to be acquired by MTN Group at $8.50/share cash (~$6.2B EV) under a signed Feb-17-2026 merger agreement, closing expected 2026 pending an EGM shareholder vote and regulatory approvals.
verified.dated_milestone: Extraordinary General Meeting (EGM) shareholder vote in London, scheduled for later in 2026 (2/3 of votes cast required); definitive date not yet published — next hard date pending.
verified.instrument: equity (long IHS NYSE common into the $8.50 cash takeout)
verified.bloom_gates: G1 (named counterparty MTN Group) + G2 (concrete commitment: signed binding merger agreement + voting/support agreements) + G3 (specific currently-unpriced figure: $8.50 cash vs ~$8.21 tape = the un-captured spread)
verified.primary_source: IHS Towers press release / BusinessWire "IHS Towers Announces Proposed Sale to MTN Group Limited for Approximately $6.2 Billion" (2026-02-17); status corroborated by TechCabal (2026-05-25); price FMP quote $8.205 (2026-06-29).
verified.kill_fact: Independently confirmed vs primary/credible sources (MTN release, IHS 6-K/StockTitan, TechCabal, Fried Frank): signed Feb-17-2026 merger agreement, MTN to acquire IHS at $8.50/share all-cash, ~$6.2B EV; IHS board backs deal. Voting/support agreements lock MTN 21.1% (85.2M sh) + Wendel/Oranje-Nassau ~19.6% (~63M sh) = ~40.7%; EGM needs two-thirds of votes CAST and remains undated in 2026, conditional on regulatory approvals + Latam disposals. Stock ~$8.20-8.25 = ~3.5-3.6% below $8.50 cash, consistent spread. Forward, not yet fired. Caveat: 40.7% is of total voting power, not of votes cast, so the threshold is not as de-risked as a 'locked 40%+' framing implies; spread thin and timeline open-ended.
verified.analysis: Confirmed clean cash merger-arb: MTN signed a binding Feb-17-2026 agreement to buy IHS at $8.50/share (~$6.2B EV), the IHS board has approved it, and ~40%+ of votes are locked via MTN (21.1%) and Wendel/Oranje-Nassau (19.6%) voting agreements, materially de-risking the EGM. At $8.205 the stock sits ~3.6% below the $8.50 cash price, so the spread compensates for residual risk: regulatory approvals across African markets (Nigeria especially), FX/cash-repatriation mechanics, and the still-undated 2026 EGM requiring two-thirds of votes cast. Forward and not yet fired; primary gap is that no firm EGM/closing date has been published, so the timeline to capture is open-ended within 2026.

verified.catalyst: TEBE-AM Phase 3 topline OS readout for tebentafusp (mono and +pembro) in 2L+ advanced cutaneous melanoma, in a setting where no therapy has shown OS benefit post-checkpoint.
verified.dated_milestone: TEBE-AM enrollment complete 1H-2026; topline data "as early as 2H-2026" (next date: 2H-2026)
verified.edge: M — company-guided, dated, OS-primary binary in an unmet 2L+ cutaneous-melanoma setting (no prior post-checkpoint OS win), so a hit is materially unpriced; but "as early as 2H-2026" is soft guidance (could slip), and uveal-melanoma KIMMTRAK base is already commercial/known so downside is partly buffered, capping the edge.
verified.primary_source: Immunocore JPM 2026 strategic-priorities PR via StockTitan, dated 2026-01-09 (TEBE-AM topline "as early as 2H 2026"); AACR 5-yr OS presented 2026-04-19 (already fired)
verified.analysis: IMCR is commercial-stage on KIMMTRAK (uveal melanoma) with a genuine dated forward binary: TEBE-AM Phase 3 topline (OS primary, tebentafusp mono +/- pembro, 2L+ advanced cutaneous melanoma) guided to "as early as 2H-2026" per the Jan 9, 2026 JPM strategic-priorities release, enrollment to complete 1H-2026. This clears Bloom G3 — a specific currently-unpriced OS figure in a setting with no prior post-checkpoint OS win — and is forward/not-fired. Note the triage flag conflated molecules: the cutaneous-melanoma topline is tebentafusp (TEBE-AM), not brenetafusp (which is the first-line PRISM-MEL301 trial, no 2026 readout); and the tebentafusp 5-yr uveal OS data already FIRED at AACR on Apr 19, 2026 (16% 5-yr OS), so that leg is in the past and re-rated, not a forward catalyst. WATCH/score 6 is appropriate: one real soft-dated 2H-2026 binary, no firm date, partial downside buffer from the existing commercial franchise.

verified.catalyst: FDA decision on the varegacestat NDA for adult desmoid tumors — a dated regulatory binary in a small orphan indication with one approved competitor (Ogsiveo/nirogacestat).
verified.dated_milestone: NDA submitted 2026-04-29; next dated event = FDA filing/acceptance decision (~60-day clock, late June 2026) which sets the PDUFA action date (PDUFA itself not yet disclosed — likely standard ~10-mo review → Q1-Q2 2027, or 6-mo if priority → ~Q4 2026).
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete regulatory commitment — NDA accepted-for-filing pending, definitive FDA action to follow); partial G1 (named counterparty = FDA) and G3 (definable orphan TAM vs Ogsiveo).
verified.edge: M — Phase 3 data is overwhelmingly positive (HR 0.16, ORR 56% vs 9%, 84% risk reduction) and already presented at ASCO/largely priced, so the approval probability is high but the upside is the launch/label, not a surprise; edge is capped because the data is fired and the exact PDUFA date is undisclosed, leaving timing un-pinnable.
verified.primary_source: Immunome press release "Submission of NDA to U.S. FDA for Varegacestat" via BusinessWire/investors.immunome.com, dated 2026-04-29; ASCO RINGSIDE detail 2026-05-30; orphan designation (as AL102) 2023.
verified.analysis: Immunome submitted the varegacestat NDA on 2026-04-29 off positive Phase 3 RINGSIDE data (HR 0.16, 84% reduction in progression/death risk, 56% ORR vs 9%), with orphan drug designation held since 2023 and only one approved competitor in desmoid tumors. The forward FDA decision is a genuine dated regulatory binary that is not yet fired, but the exact PDUFA action date has NOT been disclosed and no priority-review grant or NDA acceptance has been announced as of the latest news (2026-06-11), so the milestone is real but not yet pinned to a date. The Phase 3 readout is already public/ASCO-presented and substantially priced, which caps the edge — this is a high-probability approval whose surprise value lies in label/launch terms rather than the binary outcome, warranting WATCH rather than ACTIVE until the FDA accepts the filing and assigns a PDUFA date.

verified.catalyst: SUPRAME Phase 3 event-triggered interim (and final) PFS readout for anzu-cel/IMA203 PRAME TCR-T in 2L+ metastatic melanoma, with pivotal BLA to follow.
verified.dated_milestone: Interim + final PFS analyses event-triggered, both expected to be triggered in 2026 (next; BLA submission 1H-2027, launch 2H-2027)
verified.instrument: equity (NASDAQ: IMTX); LEAPS/long-dated calls viable given binary profile and runway into 2028
verified.bloom_gates: G2 (concrete dated BLA commitment 1H-2027) + G3 (specific unpriced binary: pivotal BICR-assessed PFS hazard ratio vs investigator's choice)
verified.edge: M — real company-specific dated binary, fully funded into 2028 past the readout; but interim is event-triggered (no fixed date) within 2026 and PRAME platform is partly re-rated, capping asymmetry
verified.primary_source: Immatics Q1 2026 Financial Results & Business Update, May 12, 2026 (investors.immatics.com / stocktitan.net IMTX)
verified.analysis: Immatics' own Q1 2026 release (May 12, 2026) states the SUPRAME Phase 3 interim AND final PFS analyses are event-triggered (~defined PFS events, ~200-pt design) and BOTH are expected to be triggered in 2026 — making the binary more imminent than the WATCH triage's "not imminent in 2026" framing implied. It is a genuine dated, forward, unfired, company-specific binary (registrational PFS in 2L+ post-PD1 melanoma) backed by $521.5M cash (Mar 31 2026) with runway into 2028, comfortably past the readout and the planned 1H-2027 BLA. Gate passes (G2+G3); held at WATCH rather than ACTIVE because the interim trigger date is event-driven (not fixed) and PRAME-platform optionality is already partly priced.
